features.name: Upwork gig scanner: Safari automation search for gigs matching skill keywords (website, landing page, automation, SaaS), returns array of { gig_id, title, budget, description, url }
Gig feasibility scorer (10-point): landing page/website (+3), already has designs (+2), fixed price <$500 (+2), tech stack match (+2), verified payment (+1)
Build pipeline: for feasible gig (score≥7), spawn Claude Code agent via harness/run-harness-v2.js to build deliverable in /tmp/upwork-{gig-id}/
GitHub push: after build, git init + git remote add + git push to isaiahdupree/upwork-{gig-id} repo via gh CLI
Vercel deploy: npx vercel --yes --prod in /tmp/upwork-{gig-id}/ → capture live URL
Passport drive backup: cp -r /tmp/upwork-{gig-id}/ /Volumes/Passport/clients/upwork-{gig-id}/ if drive mounted
Proposal drafter: template with demo URL + GitHub link: 'I already built this. Demo: {url}. Approve to ship.' Max 500 chars.
Telegram approval queue: POST to Telegram bot with gig details + demo URL + /approve_{id} | /skip_{id} commands
Telegram webhook handler: receives /approve or /skip, updates upwork-queue.json, triggers Safari automation to send proposal
upwork-queue.json: { id, gig_id, title, budget, demo_url, github_url, proposal_text, status, created_at, approved_at }
Backend routes: GET /api/upwork/status, GET /api/upwork/queue, POST /api/upwork/queue/approve, POST /api/upwork/queue/skip
harness/prompts/upwork-builder.md: Claude Code agent prompt for building landing pages/websites from gig descriptions
